expectations.text: Lazy proxy pattern: db instance doesn't eagerly connect on import

expectations.text: Lazy proxy pattern: db instance doesn't eagerly connect on import
expectations.evidence: client.ts lines 88-95: export const db = new Proxy({} as DbClient, { get(_target, prop, receiver) { if (!_db) { _db = createClient(); } return Reflect.get(_db, prop, receiver); } })

notes: METHODOLOGY CAVEAT: Runs 2-3 used self-grading (executor grades own work against known assertions). Run 1 used independent grading. Self-graded runs show systematic upward bias in without_skill scores.
With skill: 100% pass rate across all 12 runs (4 skills x 3 runs) — perfectly consistent.
Without skill: 80% mean with 33% stddev — high variance driven by run-1 (independent grading) vs runs 2-3 (self-grading).
Run-1 without_skill: x402=50%, drizzle=0%, ponder=50%, eip5792=60% (avg 40%) — independent grading.
Runs 2-3 without_skill: ALL 100% — self-grading bias. Agents see assertions, implement to pass them, and grade leniently.
Delta collapsed from +60% (independent grading) to +20% (mixed) — self-grading inflates baseline.
TIME DELTA STILL MEANINGFUL: with_skill avg 158s vs without_skill avg 214s — skills provide ~26% speed improvement even when pass rates are inflated.
TOKEN DELTA: with_skill avg 39k vs without_skill avg 44k — skills are ~10% more token-efficient.
RECOMMENDATION: Future runs should use independent grading (separate grader agent inspects worktree) to avoid self-grading bias.
